---
name: Suggest a new AI Slop Tell / Cliché
about: Propose a default AI-generated pattern or visual/textual cliché to filter out.
title: "[TELL] "
labels: suggestion, tell
assignees: ''

---

**Tell/Cliché Category**
Select which category this tell belongs to:
- [ ] Visual Clichés (e.g. gradients, floating dots, bento grids, layout rhythm)
- [ ] Color & Typography Defaults (e.g. purple-to-blue palettes, default tracking/letter-spacing, font choices)
- [ ] Copywriting Clichés (e.g. "unlock", "supercharge", "rule-of-three" adjectives, em-dash heavy)
- [ ] UX & Information Architecture Defaults (e.g. 3-step 'how it works', pricing layout, empty states)
- [ ] Source Code Tells (e.g. `transition: all 0.3s ease`, div soup, layout magic numbers)
- [ ] Infrastructure & DevOps Tells (e.g. Dockerfile `latest` tag, hardcoded regions, insecure defaults)
- [ ] Documentation Tells (e.g. empty README, boilerplate getting started)

**Describe the Tell / Cliché**
Please describe the pattern or behavior in detail. What visual/code/copy elements make up this tell?

**Why is it an AI Default?**
Why do you believe this is a statistical average or default generated by LLMs (e.g. Claude, GPT, Gemini, v0) rather than a deliberate human design decision? (Please include screenshots, generated examples, or code snippets if possible).

**Proposed Checklist Wording**
How should this tell be phrased when added to the guide (`docs/detecting-ai-slop.md`) or the skill (`skills/web-ai-slop/SKILL.md`)?
*(e.g., "- [ ] Purple-to-blue or purple-to-pink radial gradient blob behind the headline")*
